best_edges.action: Protect as the anchor baseline; do not let new-regime discovery overwrite this with broad gates.
best_edges.evidence: Leaderboard is stable across repeated runs: $7149.6 net profit, 107 trades, 35.66 exposure hours, $200.5 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE only -513.4, average MAE -100.47, no slow >=12h trades. This is far superior to run_043 despite run_043's 100% win rate.

best_edges.action: Protect, but split tail states before expanding.
best_edges.evidence: In run_043, pullback_long produced $1772.1 over 26 trades, best active profile PnL, 100% win rate, median hold 13.4m. However it also carried one slow >=12h trade and worst MAE -1985.6, so the edge exists but has a deep-heat subregime.

best_edges.action: Protect as a clean data-state edge; avoid direct tightening repeats.
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte produced $464.5 on only 7 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades, $146.0 PnL/exposure hour, avg MAE -87.3, worst MAE -327.25, no slow trades. Prior direct 1DTE quality tightening hurt score and net profit, so preserve baseline and study companion splits only.

best_edges.action: Protect as fallback insurance, especially expired-state behavior.
best_edges.evidence: EXPIRED_0DTE fallback was small but very efficient: 3 trades, $198.9, $453.8 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -65.45. STALE_0DTE added 8 trades and $243.1 with no slow trades, though efficiency was weaker at $20.0/hour.

best_edges.action: Keep as a known convex profile family, but do not infer from run_043's zero trades alone.
best_edges.evidence: Historical memory shows squeeze TP/continuation tuning from run_018 to run_021 improved score by +2587, net profit by +$848.6, drawdown from 2.0% to 0.5%, and worst MAE by +1387.75. run_043 has zero squeeze trades because the indicator-resolution experiment changed the reachable regime, not because the concept is invalid.

broken_edges.action: Reject and quarantine; do not use run_043 as a base for expansion logic except as a pathology dataset.
broken_edges.evidence: Changing indicator-register-resolution to Second from run_040 to run_043 cut score by -15618, net profit by -$3270.8, exposure rose by +141.1 hours, PnL/exposure collapsed from $200.5/hour to $21.9/hour, drawdown jumped to 4.1%, and worst MAE deteriorated from -513.4 to -4387.7.
broken_edges.problem: Second-resolution indicator registration created a new slow-tail regime and should not be treated as a profitable regime discovery.

broken_edges.action: Do not expand; focus on split/risk attribution and tail containment.
broken_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long in run_043 produced $1200.2 but consumed 118.6 exposure hours, only $10.1/hour, with one slow trade and global worst MAE -4387.7. The worst trade held 5775.6 minutes for only $34 PnL and never reached TP in path stats.
broken_edges.problem: Profitable on closed PnL but badly capital-inefficient and tail-heavy under the new data timing regime.

broken_edges.action: Avoid repeating broad setup or structure pruning as the primary answer.
broken_edges.evidence: Candidate filters such as option_strike_map_gamma_regime_score >= 0.5 retained only 84.1% PnL; call/put imbalance >=0.5 retained 77.6%; rsi_slope >= -0.5 retained 68.4%. These reduce worst MAE but sacrifice too much throughput from an already damaged run_043 base.
broken_edges.problem: Broad filters reduce heat but do not preserve enough edge for risk-adjusted mode.

broken_edges.action: Do not optimize dead profiles from run_043 unless used as explicit activation probes.
broken_edges.evidence: squeeze_long, charm_wall_breakaway_long, call_wall_acceptance_long, breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long produced zero trades in run_043. Several are enabled but unreachable in this bad regime.
broken_edges.problem: Zero-trade profiles are not evidence of failure; they are unreachable under the latest run state and should not receive local threshold optimization without a router/sample-builder hypothesis.

broken_edges.action: Do not compress charm_wall exits again.
broken_edges.evidence: The charm-wall realization trim from run_038 to run_041 hurt score by -143.7 and net profit by -$146.7 despite improving exposure slightly. The profile appears to need convex target space when it triggers.
broken_edges.problem: Exit compression reduced portfolio objective and should not be repeated as the next new-regime experiment.

market_regime_read: The latest run is not a healthy continuation of the leader regime; it is a pathology run caused by indicator-register-resolution=Second. It converted a fast, low-MAE, high-throughput no-stop-loss system into a capital-trapping regime: exposure rose to 176.7 hours, two trades exceeded 12 hours, worst MAE reached -4387.7, and slow trades delivered only $68 total. Still, it is useful diagnostically: the bad state concentrates in VALID_0DTE pullback/charm-pin entries with weak early progress, lower ADX, negative/flat ADX slope, lower setup quality, and extended-vwap rejection near-misses. The research objective should be new-regime discovery from this pathology while preserving the run_029 leader family as the baseline.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: The worst tails are path-dependent: they fail to make early progress and later recover for tiny profits after huge capital lockup.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Can overfit a handful of delayed recoveries or block clean extended-VWAP winners unless protected winner retention is tested explicitly.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Early Progress Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path fields: first nonzero move, first +1.5/+2.0 point profit time, MFE before MAE, first-15m MFE/MAE, profile, option data state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback/charm-pin entries with no meaningful positive progress in the first minutes or MFE not preceding MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce worst MAE toward leader-family levels, remove slow >=12h trades, retain >=90% pullback/charm-pin PnL, retain >=95% protected fast winners, and keep total score flat or better versus run_029 base.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: 1DTE fallback remains efficient but likely contains breakout and pullback/reclaim subregimes.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the companion may be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades with ES pullback/reclaim state, option map validity, ADX/RSI, VWAP distance, and profile attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current 1DTE baseline, average/worst 1DTE MAE improves, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL is measured, and total net profit remains >=98% of leader.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Charm/pin risk may be better split into pin-avoid concepts than filtered globally.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant activation can steal good charm_pin_pullback_long entries without enough standalone sample.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Router Split Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router-level attribution among charm_pin_pullback_long, charm_wall_breakaway_long, late_day_pin_avoid_long, and charm_wall_pin_avoid_long; protected fast-winner retention.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pin/charm-wall context near pin-risk or wall-pin-avoid states where existing charm_pin_pullback_long accepts high heat.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit stays >=98% of leader and protected fast-winner retention remains high.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Expired no-0DTE fallback is highly efficient and may deserve a dedicated state split, while stale fallback is slower.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Existing router only exposes no-0DTE list, so separating EXPIRED_0DTE from STALE_0DTE may require code if state-specific routing is unavailable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Expired-vs-Stale No-0DTE Router Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Option data state, selected no-0DTE profile, trade timing, stale age, fallback PnL/exposure, MAE, and hold time.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE data unavailable but state differs between EXPIRED_0DTE and STALE_0DTE.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Preserve EXPIRED_0DTE efficiency, improve STALE_0DTE PnL/exposure and MAE, keep VALID_0DTE unchanged, and maintain total score near leader.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Bad rows were all covered by an enabled extended-VWAP rejection near-miss signature, implying thresholds, not concept, are the gap.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Static widening may block clean extended-VWAP winners, especially the 8 protected extended-VWAP winners with $302.6 PnL and median hold 4.6m.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Profile-Aware Extended-VWAP Near-Miss Veto Study
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Extended-VWAP signature threshold misses, protected extended-VWAP winners, profile attribution, MAE, PnL, and hold time.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Entries matching extended-VWAP rejection geometry but missing by one boundary such as higher-high distance, RSI slope, VWAP distance, or pullback-from-high.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves materially, slow >=12h trades are eliminated or reduced, retained total PnL >=98% of leader, and protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=95%.

next_experiments.config_bias: Restore the leader's indicator timing assumptions and run only one small discovery family at a time. Treat run_043 diagnostics as labels, not as the candidate base behavior to preserve.
next_experiments.goal: Confirm that the next experiment is evaluated against the run_029 leader regime and not the run_043 pathology.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The optimizer should use run_043 only to identify bad-state signatures; the candidate base should remain best-by-score run_029 because indicator-register-resolution=Second destroyed the risk-adjusted objective.
next_experiments.name: Leader Baseline Guardrail
next_experiments.primary_metric: Risk-adjusted score, exposure hours, worst MAE, slow_12h trades, and net profit versus run_029.
next_experiments.risk: If the optimizer accidentally inherits run_043's timing state, all subsequent profile conclusions will be polluted.

next_experiments.config_bias: Test one dormant valid-0DTE pin-avoid concept through a full router-list replacement with minimal same-profile tuning. Prefer attribution over aggressiveness; do not remove core pullback/charm-pin profiles in the first probe.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether pin-avoid routing can separate charm-pin tail risk without global veto pruning.
next_experiments.hypothesis: charm_pin_pullback_long is profitable but tail-heavy; a dormant late_day_pin_avoid_long or charm_wall_pin_avoid_long split may capture hazardous pin/charm contexts while preserving fast pullback winners.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Split Attribution
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/pin-avoid worst MAE, protected fast-winner retention, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample and router cannibalization; require explicit comparison of charm_pin_pullback_long retained PnL and protected UTCs.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte thresholds stable. If legal, add pullback_long_1dte to the one-dte router list as a companion split; do not tighten the existing breakout fallback directly.
next_experiments.goal: Separate 1DTE pullback/reclaim trades from breakout continuation without damaging the known 1DTE edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The 1DTE fallback has clean efficiency, while past direct tightening hurt; a companion profile can improve regime attribution and MAE without starving breakout_long_1dte.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Router Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, breakout_long_1dte cannibalization, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: The companion profile may be unreachable or may steal the best breakout trades; require side-by-side attribution.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use narrow profile-aware boundary research around the existing extended-VWAP rejection signature. Avoid broad setup-quality, RSI-slope, gamma-score, or call/put floors unless retained PnL stays near leader quality.
next_experiments.goal: Convert the run_043 bad-MAE near-miss evidence into a surgical risk gate that does not block clean extended-VWAP winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The enabled extended-VWAP rejection veto nearly captured all deep-MAE rows, so small threshold-boundary studies should outperform broad pruning.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Near-Miss Boundary Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, slow_12h count, protected extended-VWAP winner retention, and total risk-adjusted score.
next_experiments.risk: The protected extended-VWAP winners share some high-extension features; blunt VWAP caps can destroy a real edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Study no-0DTE fallback by data state. If config-only separation is unavailable, run a conservative holdout that measures STALE_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE separately rather than optimizing aggregate fallback stats.
next_experiments.goal: Preserve the very efficient EXPIRED_0DTE fallback while reducing slower STALE_0DTE capital usage.
next_experiments.hypothesis: EXPIRED_0DTE pullback_no_0dte is an edge, while STALE_0DTE is lower efficiency; treating them together hides the better state.
next_experiments.name: No-0DTE Data-State Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: EXPIRED_0DTE PnL/exposure, STALE_0DTE PnL/exposure, fallback worst MAE, VALID_0DTE unchanged, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: Only 11 fallback trades in run_043; any conclusion is fragile and must not damage VALID_0DTE routing.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not compress exits. Rebuild sample only through tiny admission/routing attribution probes if the profile is reachable on the leader baseline.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether charm_wall_breakaway_long remains a convex edge or was made unreachable by current routing.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Historical charm-wall trades were high convexity, but recent exit compression hurt; the next useful test is reachability and attribution, not TP reduction.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Reachability Attribution
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long trade count, standalone PnL/exposure, total score, and worst MAE.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample; loosening admission can add weak wall-chase entries, while exit trimming has already shown negative objective impact.

research_notes: The most important inference is that run_043 is a failed upstream timing experiment, not a new profitable market regime. Use its bad-MAE fingerprints for research labels only. The best next new-regime work is split discovery: dormant pin-avoid activation, 1DTE companion routing, extended-VWAP near-miss repair, and expired-vs-stale fallback separation. Avoid broad filters that only look good inside run_043 because their retained PnL is far below the leader family. Also avoid further charm-wall exit compression and direct 1DTE tightening, both of which have already hurt objective results.
risk_controls: Promote no experiment unless total net profit remains near the run_029 leader and score is flat or better under risk-adjusted mode.
Require slow_12h trades to remain 0 or improve versus the evaluated baseline; any new slow trade is a rejection flag for no-stop-loss optimization.
Track worst MAE and average MAE separately; a profitable all-winner run can still be invalid if exposure hours and MAE explode.
For dormant router activations, measure standalone PnL and cannibalization of existing profiles, not just total PnL.
Protect fast winners and low-MAE winners; do not accept a filter that removes many protected UTCs without materially reducing tail MAE.
Never optimize from indicator-register-resolution=Second as if it were a valid leader-family config.
